title: Witness & Whistleblower Database 1.0
description: Documented profiles connecting people to congressional testimony, interviews, organizations, hearings, claims, and supporting source material.

profiles.summary: Former U.S. intelligence officer whose public testimony and written statement raised allegations concerning hidden UAP programs and congressional oversight.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of public congressional testimony, written testimony, public interviews, and documented whistleblower-related reporting.

profiles.status: Former official, public witness, and disclosure advocate
profiles.summary: Former Department of Defense official who testified publicly about alleged secret UAP programs, oversight concerns, and protections for people reporting UAP information.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of public congressional testimony, an official written statement, and testimony concerning whistleblowers and alleged retaliation.

profiles.status: Self-identified federal whistleblower and congressional witness
profiles.summary: Former Air Force geospatial-intelligence specialist who described himself in official written testimony as a federal whistleblower and presented allegations concerning UAP programs and retaliation.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because his official House statement explicitly identifies him as a federal whistleblower and records testimony to AARO and the Intelligence Community Inspector General.

profiles.status: Firsthand military witness and congressional witness
profiles.summary: Active-duty U.S. Navy Senior Chief Petty Officer who submitted a firsthand account of a February 2023 UAP event and discussed it publicly in congressional testimony and an interview.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included as a witness profile, not labeled as a whistleblower. His record tests how witness testimony, interviews, claims, organizations, and supporting documents connect.

profiles.status: Former military officer and public disclosure advocate
profiles.summary: Retired U.S. Army colonel, aerospace executive, and public advocate for greater government disclosure concerning UAP.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of his former military and national-security background, public statements concerning UAP disclosure, and direct relevance to claims and people already represented in the knowledge graph.

profiles.status: Former government official and public disclosure advocate
profiles.summary: Former Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense for Intelligence and former Senate Intelligence Committee staff director who has advocated for congressional UAP oversight.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of his former defense and congressional-intelligence roles, public advocacy for UAP oversight, and involvement in bringing military UAP reporting to congressional attention.

profiles.status: Firsthand military witness and congressional witness
profiles.summary: Former U.S. Navy F/A-18 pilot, aviation-safety advocate, and congressional witness.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of firsthand military reporting, official congressional testimony, written testimony, and continuing aviation-safety advocacy.

profiles.status: Firsthand military witness and congressional witness
profiles.summary: Retired U.S. Navy commander, firsthand witness to the 2004 Nimitz encounter, and congressional witness.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of firsthand involvement in the 2004 Nimitz encounter, official congressional testimony, written testimony, and documented public interviews.

profiles.status: Former government official and congressional witness
profiles.summary: Former Director of the All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office and government witness before Congress.
profiles.inclusionNote: Included because of his leadership of AARO, public and classified congressional testimony, and central role in the official government response to UAP reporting and analysis.
